regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 17, 2026, shows light east to east-southeast trades with moderate seas and mainly stable conditions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos. Winds will increase slightly to 14-16 knots from the east today with partly cloudy skies and isolated showers less than 20 percent chance, reducing chances over the next two days. Mariners should expect comfortable anchorage conditions in all ports, though moderate easterly winds call for secure mooring lines, especially in exposed harbors like St. Thomas and St. Maarten. Fishing captains are advised to monitor sea state as winds pick up, but no marine warnings are expected; passengers should plan for pleasant weather with minimal rain interruptions.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean briefing for March 17, 2026: Cozumel reports 78°F with light NNW winds at 3 knots and isolated thunderstorms; rain chance 28 percent. A strong weather system will bring thunderstorms and near gale-force NNE winds up to 20 knots today, especially affecting Cozumel and Costa Maya, with a 98 percent rain probability. Expect improving conditions by March 19 with overcast skies and moderate N winds at 14 knots. Mariners should secure loose gear and avoid exposed anchorages in Cozumel and Costa Maya during the peak thunderstorms; fishing captains should plan for reduced visibility and choppy seas early today. Cruise passengers at Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth should anticipate scattered showers but generally lighter winds and safer conditions compared to the westernmost ports.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 17, 2026: Current conditions in Nassau show 76°F with southerly winds at 8 knots and isolated thunderstorms, though rain chances remain low at 13%. Expect increasing thunderstorm activity today with west-northwest winds around 13 knots and nearly 50% probability of rain, especially near Nassau and Freeport. By March 18-19, northerly winds will pick up to 10-20 knots with frequent rain showers and rain chances near 80%, raising the likelihood of rougher seas and reduced visibility around Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos. Small craft operators and fishing captains should prepare for choppy conditions and consider sheltering in well-protected anchorages; cruise passengers should anticipate wet weather and brief thunderstorm delays during port excursions.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean weather for March 17, 2026, features steady east-southeast winds near 22 knots at Aruba with temperatures around 80 to 82 degrees Fahrenheit and partly cloudy skies. Conditions remain generally stable with low rain chances through the next three days; however, light rain showers may develop by day three, mainly affecting offshore waters. Mariners operating near Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should anticipate moderate easterly trade winds and seas, warranting caution for small craft, especially in exposed anchorages. Cruise and fishing captains are advised to secure gear and monitor for scattered showers late in the week, while passengers should plan light wind protection and sun safety during daytime activities.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today are stable with temperatures near 80°F and easterly winds at 9 knots. Skies will remain partly cloudy with no rain expected at Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Cozumel, or Costa Maya. Mariners should note that increasing moisture and east-southeast winds will bring scattered showers and thunderstorms from tomorrow through March 19, with rain chances rising above 90 percent and winds shifting to the north-northeast near 15 knots by day three. Captains operating near Grand Cayman and surrounding anchorages should prepare for rougher seas and reduced visibility due to thunderstorms; delay fishing or small craft operations during peak storm activity and ensure passengers remain in secure areas when at port.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles ports report steady ENE trade winds 15-21 knots with partly cloudy skies and low rain chances through March 17-19. Barbados currently 78°F with ENE 20 kt winds and a 15% rain threat; similar conditions expected at St. Lucia, Martinique, and St. Vincent. Small Craft Advisories may be warranted for exposed anchorages in Antigua and St. Maarten due to sustained winds near 20 kt; caution advised when maneuvering or operating smaller vessels. Cruise passengers should plan for mild sea conditions and limited rain, but keep light rain gear available due to isolated showers.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and adjacent ports will see light northwest winds near 1 knot with clear skies and no precipitation this morning. Expect a warm day with west winds increasing to 10 knots and partly cloudy conditions, easing docking and maneuvering in Los Angeles/Long Beach harbors. Tonight, fog will reduce visibility around Marina del Rey and Catalina Island, so exercise caution when navigating or anchoring after dark. Fishing captains should plan for calm seas and light winds through Wednesday, with no marine warnings in effect.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region is currently mild with light WNW winds near 2 knots and partly cloudy skies; no precipitation expected today. Fog will develop tonight and persist through tomorrow morning across Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos, reducing visibility—vessels should exercise caution entering or leaving port during these hours. Winds will remain light from the WSW at 6-7 knots with calm seas, ideal for small craft operations and fishing activities. By day three, expect a significant warm-up to near 91°F with clear skies; ensure adequate sun protection and hydration for passengers and crew during extended outdoor activities.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will see stable conditions today with temperatures near 72 to 74 degrees Fahrenheit. Winds remain steady from the northwest at 12 to 13 knots, generating moderate sea conditions but no Small Craft Advisories are expected. Skippers should maintain normal precautions when approaching anchorage areas, particularly in La Paz where northwest winds may funnel through the bay. Passengers can expect partly cloudy skies with no chance of rain, making it ideal for outdoor activities and fishing trips.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region is currently experiencing calm conditions with clear skies, light northerly winds at 1 knot, and temperatures near 73 degrees Fahrenheit. Over the next three days, expect gentle WNW to WSW winds between 6 and 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and no precipitation. Mariners can anticipate smooth seas ideal for fishing or recreational cruising; however, small craft should monitor local sea state in Altata Bay where shallower waters can amplify chop. Cruise passengers should enjoy pleasant weather ashore with comfortable temperatures and minimal UV risk this week.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region is enjoying calm and clear conditions this morning with light northeast winds at 1 knot and no rain expected. Winds will shift west-northwest and increase to 6-8 knots through the next three days with partly cloudy skies and stable weather, suitable for all marine activities. Captains should expect smooth seas and excellent visibility at all ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala; minimal wind-driven current changes are anticipated, making anchoring straightforward. Cruise and fishing operations can proceed as planned with no marine warnings in effect.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 17, 2026: Barcelona currently reports clear skies, light northwest winds at 3 knots, and a cool 50°F with no rain expected today. Expect fog early tomorrow morning near Valencia and Palma de Mallorca with WSW winds around 5 knots; visibilities may be reduced for vessels entering port—exercise caution during docking. By day two, increasing easterly winds to 14 knots will bring scattered rain showers near Ibiza and Malaga, though rain chances remain low. Yacht and fishing captains should monitor shifting winds and overcast conditions on day three, especially around Cadiz and Gibraltar, with ENE winds near 11 knots and minimal rain; no marine warnings are in effect. Passengers should dress in layers and prepare for variable conditions when ashore.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean forecast for March 17, 2026: Naples reports overcast skies with northeast winds at 10 knots and a 28 percent chance of rain this morning. Expect northeast winds to increase to 12 knots with intermittent rain through the day and a 48 percent rain probability. Conditions will remain unsettled with lighter northerly winds and scattered showers over the next two days. Mariners should prepare for reduced visibility and slick decks; yacht captains should monitor anchorage conditions near ports like Genoa and Civitavecchia, where rain and moderate winds may affect mooring security. Cruise passengers should carry weather gear ashore and anticipate possible itinerary adjustments due to wet weather.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 17, 2026:

Currently in Athens/Piraeus, temperatures hold steady at 54°F with light northerly winds near 2 knots under overcast skies and no rain expected today. Over the next three days, expect increasing northeasterly winds up to 9 knots with a rising chance of rain showers peaking at 50% on March 18. Mariners should exercise routine caution in ports such as Rhodes, Mykonos, and Santorini where localized showers may reduce visibility and increase deck slickness. Yacht and fishing captains are advised to secure gear and monitor updated forecasts before heading offshore, especially near Istanbul and Izmir where showers could impact sea conditions. Passengers should carry rain gear for excursions in Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria as intermittent rain is likely.
